name: Sweet and salty cheesecake with cherries
intro: the crumble (which keeps a good week or so) just needs to be kept in an airtight

ingredients.item: oz/300g full-fat cream cheese, at room temperature
small lemon,  nely zested to get 1 tsp
unsalted butter, fridge-cold and cut into ¾-inch/2cm dice
orange: peel  nely shaved to get 4 strips

method: Using a spatula, break the feta down in a large bowl to make it as smooth as possible. Add the cream cheese, sugar, and lemon zest and whisk to combine. Pour in the cream and gently whisk until the mixture has thickened enough to hold its shape. Leave to set in the fridge until ready to use.

To make the crumble, place the hazelnuts, butter, almond meal, and sugar in a bowl. Use the tips of your  ngers to rub the butter into everything else until the consistency of breadcrumbs is formed. Stir in the sesame seeds and salt, then spread out on a baking sheet. Bake for about 12 minutes, until golden brown.

To make the compote, put the cherries, sugar, star anise, and orange peel into a medium saucepan and place over medium-high heat. Bring to a boil, then simmer for 10–15 minutes, until the sauce has thickened (but it will continue to thicken as it cools down). Set aside to come to room temperature. Discard star anise and orange peel.

When ready to serve, spoon a large scoop of the cheesecake into each bowl and top with some crumble. Spoon the compote on top and  nish with the remaining crumble. Drizzle with the olive oil and serve.
Vanilla custard with roasted strawberries and rhubarb
If it’s in season, use forced rhubarb here: the hot-pink color of the slender stalks looks stunning once cooked. Regular eld-grown rhubarb, though, is also great.
The custard and fruit mixtures can be made a day in advance and kept in the fridge until ready to assemble. Serve this with some shortbread alongside, if you’re looking for crunch.
